response.text:
## REFLECTION

r1c2 is in a critical survival state: islanded with SoC at 0.37/4 kWh (9.25%), a 1.00 kW load, and approximately 22 minutes of autonomy remaining. The memory log reveals a cluster-wide crisis where all peers are below 15% SoC and rejecting outbound requests; r1c2 itself has been rejected repeatedly by r0c2, r1c1, and r2c2 over the past 90 minutes. The cluster appears to be in inbound-only mode awaiting aggregator emergency supply. Simultaneously, r1c2 is receiving urgent requests from r1c3 (SoC ~0.34) and normal requests from r0c2 (SoC ~0.34–0.36), but cannot ethically or sustainably fulfill them given the imminent depletion window and lack of incoming supply. The previous policy (conservative, 0.6 SoC buffer) has proven inadequate for this cascading failure scenario. A shift toward strict survival mode—prioritizing inbound aggregator requests and load shedding negotiation—is essential to avoid blackout.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.95
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r0c2, r1c1, r2c2]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster-wide critical reserve; r1c2 islanded with 22-min depletion window. All peers rejecting outbound; inbound-only mode active. Suspending all peer sharing to preserve load continuity. Escalating to aggregator for emergency supply and load-shedding coordination."
ttl_ticks: 22
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
